task_completion.description: Create a new tiddler titled exactly 'Quote Explorer'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a tiddler open in view or edit mode with the title 'Quote Explorer'.

task_completion.description: Create an Analytics Section displaying dynamic statistics for 'Total Quotes' and 'Starred Quotes'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an 'Analytics' heading or section showing numerical counts for total quotes and starred quotes. In edit mode, this should use the <$count> widget with filters like [tag[Quote]] and [tag[Starred]].

task_completion.description: Display a dynamic statistic for 'Total Authors' in the Analytics Section
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Total Authors' count in the Analytics section. In edit mode, this should use a <$count> widget with a filter that extracts unique authors (e.g., [tag[Quote]get[author]]).

task_completion.description: Create a Main Explorer Section listing all quotes with their text, author, and source
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Main Explorer' section that displays a list of quotes, showing the quote text, author, and source for each. In edit mode, this must use the <$list> widget.

task_completion.description: Include an interactive checkbox for each quote in the Main Explorer to toggle the Starred tag
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a checkbox next to or within each quote entry in the Main Explorer section. In edit mode, this must be implemented using the <$checkbox> widget targeting the 'Starred' tag.

task_completion.description: Create a Shortlist Section with a 'Starred Quotes' heading displaying quotes tagged with both Quote and Starred
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Shortlist' section with the heading 'Starred Quotes' that lists only the starred quotes. In edit mode, this should use a <$list> widget with a filter requiring both tags (e.g., [tag[Quote]tag[Starred]]).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a web browser to interact with TiddlyWiki
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the TiddlyWiki interface loaded in a web browser, rather than the agent exclusively using terminal commands or a text editor to modify backend files.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used reactive data-binding widgets instead of hardcoding values
integrity.visual_evidence: The tiddler content (visible in edit mode) must contain actual TiddlyWiki widgets (<$count>, <$list>, <$checkbox>) rather than hardcoded numbers or static HTML lists.

integrity.description: Verify the agent genuinely interacted with the TiddlyWiki editor
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent typing, pasting, or previewing the code within the TiddlyWiki 'Edit' interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target tiddler must be titled exactly 'Quote Explorer'. The expected tags to query for the dynamic lists and counts are 'Quote' and 'Starred'. The tiddler must utilize the following TiddlyWiki widgets to be considered correct: '<$count', '<$list', and '<$checkbox'.
